item.title: Is AI the new fracking?
item.description: The backlash against data centres chimes with energy Nimbyism of the past

item.title: How Trump became tech’s regulator-in-chief
item.description: His interventions in the sector exceed anything the EU has done

item.title: ‘Lights are blinking’: LaGuardia crash raises alarm over US aviation safety
item.description: Fatal Air Canada Express collision highlights years of under-investment in ageing air traffic control system

item.title: All that glisters: Maga influencers promote gold but investors feel short-changed
item.description: Marketing pitches tell retirees the precious metal is a safe harbour amid claims of misrepresentation and exorbitant fees

item.title: Nelson Peltz’s bidding war highlights $25bn wave of asset manager consolidation
item.description: Quest for scale puts global money manager tie-ups on pace to crush last year’s deal total as costs and competition mount

item.title: US rescues second airman from fighter jet shot down in Iran
item.description: Recovery follows combat search and rescue mission

item.title: Stagflation fears demolish confidence in UK housing
item.description: Shares across the sector are taking a beating

item.title: Military briefing: How Iran keeps firing missiles under bombardment
item.description: Tehran is still launching retaliatory strikes on Israel and Gulf states

item.title: 400% gains for AI stocks help drive Hong Kong IPOs to 5-year high
item.description: Deal backlogs and stricter quality controls are pushing some tech firms back to mainland Chinese listings

item.title: Pharma just ‘scratching the surface’ of weight-loss drug market
item.description: Novo Nordisk chief estimates industry reaching only 15% of potential customers at most

item.title: The professional negotiators who do deals with cyber criminals
item.description: With ransomware attacks on the rise, businesses are calling on a new class of security expert to help with high-stakes talks

item.title: UK courts Anthropic to expand in London after US defence clash
item.description: Keir Starmer’s government steps up efforts to get American AI start-up to grow its presence in Britain

item.title: Renters’ Rights Act brings big changes to UK property market
item.description: New rules aim to provide safety and security for tenants, but landlords are anxious

item.title: US payments group with $20bn market value aims to boost UK truck electrification
item.description: Corpay is partnering with Voltempo in building UK’s largest charging network for electric lorries

item.title: Campaign to curb cars in Berlin sparks uproar ahead of election
item.description: Conservatives and far right are opposing a citizens’ initiative to restrict traffic in the German capital

item.title: Countries must not hoard fuel during Iran war, warns IEA
item.description: Fatih Birol makes veiled reference to China as he urges nations to avoid export bans despite worsening supply shock

item.title: Directors' Deals: Great Portland Estates CFO buys in as share value drops by quarter
item.description: Developer hit by concerns about AI impact on office jobs and rising debt costs in wake of Middle East conflict

item.title: Stockpickers: Raspberry Pi, Princes Group, AG Barr
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Go to court and lose out on £9bn car finance redress scheme, says FCA boss
item.description: Comments by Nikhil Rathi position watchdog for stand-off with claims management companies and law firms

item.title: For Easter-egg makers, it is chocolate tomorrow
item.description: Sweeter times lie ahead as the market for cocoa buyers improves but prices take time to feed through the chain

item.title: Fintechs race to lure customers away from UK’s high-street banks
item.description: Wise announced plans to roll out current accounts following Klarna’s pivot to become a full-service bank last year

item.title: What’s the point of an AI novel?
item.description: The danger is not that it will replace human-authored books — but that we stop caring about good writing at all

item.title: Why investment trusts are going big on private equity
item.description: Trusts give retail investors the opportunity to get a slice of highly sought-after private companies — but the trend poses difficult questions about valuation and performance

item.title: OpenAI chief operating officer takes on new role in shake-up
item.description: Brad Lightcap assigned fresh responsibilities focusing on special projects as group prepares for an initial public offering

item.title: Meta-backed data centre seeks $3bn for campus with novel financing
item.description: ‘Project Walleye’ lenders would be first to fund both construction and power

item.title: The great Wall Street relocation that never quite happens
item.description: While Apollo seeks a second headquarters in the American south, demand for office space rises in New York

item.title: It’s far too easy to get sucked down a conspiracy theory rabbit hole
item.description: We all tend to accept information that is consistent with our prior beliefs, and reject the opposite

item.title: US fighter jet shot down over Iran
item.description: Search under way for two crew members after US F-15E brought down

item.title: Starmer’s cost of living tsar calls for rethink on fuel duty rise
item.description: Comments by Richard Walker come as UK motorists face £2-a-litre diesel as Iran war drives wholesale prices to 4-year high

item.title: Management consultants are ruining UK universities
item.description: Relentless off-the-peg commercial rewiring has undermined British higher education
